relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A glory-lit messenger descends.
Babylon is announced fallen.
Nations, kings, and merchants were intoxicated by luxury-power.
A voice says: come out, my people.
Her injustices reach heaven.
Her works return upon her.
Self-glorifying luxury meets grief.
Her collapse comes because Divine judgment is strong.
Kings grieve from a distance.
The strong city falls in an hour.
Merchants weep because no one buys the cargo.
Luxury goods are named.
The list ends with bodies and human lives.
Desired splendor disappears.
Merchants stand far away grieving.
They mourn the jeweled city.
Sea-traders watch wealth made desolate.
They ask what city was like her.
Dust and lament mark the collapse of profit.
Heaven and witnesses are told to rejoice over justice.
A millstone shows Babylon thrown down.
Music, craft, and millstone cease.
Lamp, wedding voice, and city enchantment cease.
In her is found the life-blood of prophets, holy ones, and earth’s slain.

contemplative_note: Revelation 18 is the economic apocalypse of Babylon: luxury exposed as human-life commerce, and the voice says, come out, my people.
